steps.title: Find the y-intercept
steps.content: The y-intercept is the value of $f(t)$ when $t = 0$. Substitute: $$f(0) = 8{,}000(0.65)^{0} = 8{,}000 \times 1 = 8{,}000$$

steps.title: Interpret $t=0$
steps.content: Since $t$ counts years since the end of 1998, $t = 0$ corresponds to the end of 1998. So the y-intercept of 8,000 represents the number of coupons sent at the end of 1998.

steps.title: Check the choices
steps.content:
Evaluate all choices:
  • A: The minimum estimated number of coupons the company sent to their customers during the $5$ years was $1,428$. Says the minimum over 5 years was 1,428. The y-intercept is an initial value, not a minimum; and 1,428 is not 8,000. Incorrect.
  • B: The minimum estimated number of coupons the company sent to their customers during the $5$ years was $8,000$. Says the minimum over 5 years was 8,000. The y-intercept is an initial (starting) value, not a minimum; the function decreases, so the minimum is at $t=5$, not $t=0$. Incorrect.
  • C: The estimated number of coupons the company sent to their customers at the end of $1998$ was $1,428$. Says the number of coupons at the end of 1998 was 1,428. The correct y-intercept value is 8,000, not 1,428. Incorrect.
  • D: The estimated number of coupons the company sent to their customers at the end of $1998$ was $8,000$. Says the estimated number of coupons at the end of 1998 was 8,000. This correctly identifies both the time ($t=0$ = end of 1998) and the value (8,000). That matches Choice D — The estimated number of coupons the company sent to their customers at the end of $1998$ was $8,000$..
